sources.label: F-Secure investor relations / releases (no new stock-exchange release Jun 6-9, 2026; Investor Deep Dive was Jun 4)
F-Secure strategic partnership with leading Communication Service Provider (Dec 2025 release; consumer launch expected late Q2/Q3 2026)

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since Jun 6 and a flat tape (-0.24% today, +9.6% YTD); the scaling-page case (endpoint/consumer security via embedded CSP distribution) is unchanged from yesterday's hold, so rank 31 stands.
verdicts.bull: The December 2025 strategic partnership with one of the world's largest CSPs puts F-Secure's embedded security in front of 100M+ end customers, with the consumer launch due late Q2/Q3 2026 - a near-dated catalyst the market is not pricing at a $0.45B cap.
verdicts.neutral: A consumer/endpoint security vendor is adjacent to, not inside, the AI-compute bottleneck; modest YTD gain with no fresh evidence means the setup simply drifts until the CSP launch or Q2 results provide data.
verdicts.bear: Consumer security is competitive and churn-prone; if the flagship CSP rollout slips past Q3 or ramps slower than guided, the small-cap rerating case stalls and the name stays a low-rank watchlist filler.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: End-customer launch of the 100M+-subscriber CSP embedded-security partnership in late Q2/Q3 2026 and any revenue-impact disclosure around Q2 earnings.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Delay or weak attach rates in the CSP rollout, plus consumer-spend softness eroding the core subscription base.
